evidence: Large multi-responsibility contracts remain common in this batch: `BatchSettingsFormProps` includes UI state, persistence triggers, preset management, prompt controls, and phase controls in one component contract (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/BatchSettingsForm.tsx`).

`useTimelineOrchestrator` exposes a very large input/output surface (drag, selection, drop, pending, endpoint, viewport, UI flags, device behaviors), indicating orchestration and UI concerns are fused (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts`).

Shot-association control logic is duplicated across sibling components with near-identical branching and optimistic tick updates (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/ShotSelectorControls.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/WorkflowControls.tsx`).

Submission flow has stacked orchestration layers passing many values through (`useFormSubmission` -> `useSubmissionHandlers` -> `useSubmissionOrchestrator`) with limited reduction of coupling (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/useFormSubmission.ts`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/useSubmissionHandlers.ts`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/useSubmissionOrchestrator.ts`).

Tooltip usage is split across two sibling entry patterns: `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` re-exports from `primitives/tooltip`, while `src/shared/components/ui/touchableTooltip.tsx` directly imports `./tooltipPrimitives`.

Gallery/lightbox UI components (`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/InfoTooltip.tsx`, `ShotActions.tsx`) use the barrel tooltip API, but `TouchableTooltip` bypasses that shared surface and composes against a separate primitive wrapper.
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: Sibling tooltip components do not consistently use one canonical primitive/export path, which increases drift risk and makes interaction behavior harder to reason about.

evidence: In `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`, `handleRegeneration` performs `supabase.from('tasks').update({ generation_created: true }).eq('id', taskId)` without checking or handling returned `error`.

Other nearby completion flows (for example `markOrchestratorFailed` in `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`) explicitly inspect update errors and convert them to typed `CompletionError`s.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: A DB write in the completion path can fail silently, while sibling paths enforce explicit error conversion and propagation.

evidence: `createGenerationFromTask` in `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` accepts `authContext?: CompletionAuthContext` and fabricates a default unauthenticated context when omitted.
`checkOrchestratorCompletion` in `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` requires a non-optional `authContext` parameter and asserts it directly.
`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` performs explicit request authentication and service-role gating before proceeding.
authorization_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Sibling completion/auth-sensitive flows use different auth contracts (optional context vs required context vs inline request auth), which weakens a uniform fail-closed boundary.

findings.summary: Travel/timeline UI relies on oversized prop/state contracts that mix domain logic, UI controls, and persistence triggers in single units.
evidence: `BatchSettingsFormProps` carries many unrelated concerns (prompt editing, phase config, mode toggles, seed/turbo controls, preset callbacks, text-before/after, read-only behavior) in one interface.
`TimelineItemProps` includes drag mechanics, selection state, duplication/delete actions, prefetch, touch behavior, and layout parameters together.

`useTimelineOrchestrator` accepts and returns broad multi-domain structures (`viewport`, `drag`, `selection`, `pending`, `drop`, `actions`, `endpoint`, `uiState`, `device`), creating a high-coupling seam.
findings.suggestion: Split these contracts into focused capability modules: timeline interaction state, shot mutation actions, and presentation-only props. Introduce a typed coordinator object per concern (interaction, mutation, rendering) and compose in parent containers instead of passing a single mega-contract.

findings.summary: Shot-selector/add-to-shot behavior is duplicated across two lightbox control components, creating drift risk and inconsistent UX behavior.
evidence: Both `ShotSelectorControls` and `WorkflowControls` implement nearly the same `handleAddWithoutPosition` flow: guard `selectedShotId`, call `onAddToShotWithoutPosition`, then trigger secondary tick + optimistic update.
Both render `ShotSelectorWithAdd` with parallel parameter wiring and very similar tick/disabled conditions.
Behavior has already diverged: `ShotSelectorControls` includes jump-to-shot and variant-add flow, while `WorkflowControls` shows the same success tooltip text but lacks that navigation path.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `useShotAssociationControls` hook or a single reusable control component that owns add/jump/tick state transitions and is parameterized only by placement/layout concerns.

findings.summary: Form submission logic is split across multiple pass-through hooks, adding indirection without a clear reduction in dependency complexity.
evidence: `useFormSubmission` builds `formStateRef` and task params, then forwards a large argument set to `useSubmissionHandlers`.
`useSubmissionHandlers` mostly forwards the same values into `useSubmissionOrchestrator` and repackages command handlers.
`useSubmissionOrchestrator` then contains both queue orchestration and prompt-generation side effects, so layering does not isolate responsibilities cleanly.
findings.suggestion: Collapse pass-through layers into one primary hook with explicit submodules only where ownership changes (e.g., task plan builder vs queue executor). Keep a minimal command interface at the top boundary and move value derivation closer to use sites.

findings.summary: Pane context combines persistence policy, device-specific lock rules, runtime visibility, dimensions, and active-task selection in one provider.
evidence: `PanesContextType` exposes many fields spanning unrelated concerns (locks, open states, dimensions, active task id, reset behavior).
`createPaneLockSetter` performs both state mutation and persistence policy branching (`isMobile`/`isTablet`/`isSmallMobile`) in the same closure.
`TasksPane` still contains lock/open synchronization logic (`onToggleLock`, `onOpenChange`) that partially duplicates context intent, indicating boundary leakage.
findings.suggestion: Split context into focused providers: `PaneLockPolicyContext`, `PaneLayoutContext`, and `PaneSelectionContext`. Keep persistence and device policy in a dedicated hook consumed by lock policy provider to reduce cross-cutting coupling.

findings.summary: Multiple components coordinate UX transitions with hardcoded timers, indicating a systemic ad-hoc interaction-state pattern.
evidence: `TimelineItem` uses a fixed 800ms timeout for drop highlight clearing.
`ShotSelectorControls` uses a fixed 2000ms timeout to clear success UI state.
`useMediaGalleryActions` coordinates delete/backfill via 100ms and 750ms timers.
`ChunkLoadErrorBoundary` reloads after a fixed 100ms delay.
findings.suggestion: Introduce shared interaction timing constants and a small transition-state utility (or reducer) so timing decisions are centralized, testable, and behaviorally consistent.

findings.summary: Generation completion path performs an unchecked DB update that can fail silently
evidence: `handleRegeneration` updates `tasks.generation_created` but does not read or branch on `{ error }` from Supabase.
Sibling orchestration updates in the same subsystem check errors and throw `CompletionError` with metadata.
findings.suggestion: Capture `{ error }` from the `generation_created` update in `handleRegeneration`; if present, throw `CompletionError` (or `toCompletionError`) with task id/context metadata to keep completion writes fail-fast and traceable.

findings.summary: Tooltip primitives are consumed through inconsistent sibling entrypoints
evidence: `TouchableTooltip` imports tooltip primitives from local `./tooltipPrimitives`.
Other UI modules consume tooltip via the shared barrel (`@/shared/components/ui/tooltip`) that re-exports from `primitives/tooltip`.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one tooltip import surface for UI consumers (prefer the shared barrel), and remove/bound alternate primitive paths unless they are explicitly versioned for different behavior.

findings.summary: Sibling completion flows use inconsistent auth precondition contracts
evidence: `createGenerationFromTask` accepts optional `authContext` and substitutes a fallback unauthenticated object before assertion.
`checkOrchestratorCompletion` requires `authContext` from the caller and asserts that required context directly.
`calculate-task-cost` enforces authenticate-and-authorize at handler entry with service-role requirement.
findings.suggestion: Make `authContext` mandatory in `createGenerationFromTask` and align complete-task internal call sites to a single explicit auth precondition pattern (no fabricated fallback context objects).
